title: Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: The Aging with Artificial Intelligence Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to integrate artificial intelligence into healthcare systems to improve senior care and support aging populations. This act likely focuses on developing AI-assisted solutions to address the challenges and opportunities arising from an aging global population.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at promoting the use of artificial intelligence within the Small Business Administration. The exact details of the act are currently unknown due to the lack of a description.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act aims to put a temporary halt on the construction of new artificial intelligence data centers. The purpose is to assess the environmental and social impact of these facilities before allowing further development.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy aims to amend an existing AI initiative act to require the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program for artificial intelligence. It likely involves creating new opportunities and incentives for AI innovation and advancement.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a proposed law aimed at regulating and overseeing the development and use of artificial intelligence. The exact details of the act are unclear as there is no available description.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act is a policy aimed at utilizing artificial intelligence technology to monitor and assess heat-related emergencies. This act aims to enhance public safety and response to heatwaves.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Homeland Security.
summary: The U.S. Customs and Border Protection will test the use of artificial intelligence at Arizona land ports to identify potential security risks. This pilot program aims to evaluate the effectiveness of an anomaly detection algorithm in helping prevent unlawful activities at the border.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act aims to support and empower small AI innovators, likely through policies or regulations that encourage growth and development in the industry. The exact details are unclear, but the title suggests a focus on helping smaller companies or individuals working with artificial intelligence.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is a proposed policy aimed at regulating the development and use of artificial intelligence in the United States. The exact content and scope of the policy are not provided, but the title suggests it may establish guidelines and rules for the creation and deployment of AI.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 is a law aimed at preventing the misuse of artificial intelligence in elections, and it likely imposes rules and penalties for AI-related election fraud. The purpose of this act is to maintain the integrity and security of electoral processes in the context of rapidly advancing AI technology.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: This policy document is related to the regulation and governance of advanced technology and artificial intelligence within a government department. It likely outlines standards, guidelines, and laws for the development and implementation of AI systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to assess and manage the potential environmental effects of artificial intelligence systems. This legislation appears to focus on mitigating AI's harm on the environment, likely outlining regulations and guidelines for the development and deployment of AI technologies.

title: American Leadership in AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Agriculture, Oversight and Government Reform, Education and Workforce, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The American Leadership in AI Act aims to promote US leadership in artificial intelligence and mitigate the risks associated with its growing influence. The act likely includes measures to support domestic AI development and establish guidelines for ethical use.

title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This policy document outlines a proposed law requiring companies to disclose when their products or services use artificial intelligence. The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act would aim to inform consumers about AI usage.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This policy, the "Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act", aims to address the financial impact of energy costs associated with AI data centers on families. The policy likely focuses on reducing or regulating these costs.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: This policy aims to address the environmental impacts caused by the development and use of artificial intelligence. The Act of 2026 is likely intended to regulate or mitigate the environmental effects associated with AI.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 aims to establish a framework for the development and regulation of artificial intelligence across various sectors. This legislation likely seeks to provide guidelines and oversight to ensure responsible AI use.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a proposed policy aimed at increasing transparency and accountability in decision-making processes involving artificial intelligence and algorithms. The policy's specific goals and details are unclear due to the lack of further information.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a law that aims to regulate and mitigate the risks associated with the development and use of artificial intelligence in the United States. This act likely focuses on establishing guidelines and oversight to ensure safe and responsible AI development and deployment.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to create an environment where financial institutions can develop and use AI technology, promoting innovation and growth. This legislation likely addresses regulations and guidelines for the use of AI in financial services to ensure safety and efficiency.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act is a policy document, but without more information its purpose and goals cannot be definitively stated. It is likely related to policies surrounding artificial intelligence in China.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy that likely pertains to the regulation or guidelines of artificial intelligence models, aiming to ensure certain standards or practices are followed. However, more information is needed to provide a more specific or detailed summary.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act aims to establish guidelines and regulations for AI development and implementation to increase public trust.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to promote the inclusion of artificial intelligence education in schools to prepare students for a technology-driven future. This act seeks to enhance the abilities of students to navigate and understand AI.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act is a law designed to stop artificial intelligence systems from censoring certain content, aiming to ensure free and open access to information. It likely seeks to establish guidelines for AI to promote transparency and prevent biases in content moderation and filtering processes.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: The Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act is a policy document aimed at assessing the potential risks of generative AI in relation to terrorism. The document likely examines possible uses of AI technology that could facilitate or support terrorist activities.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This document proposes a bill to limit employers' use of automated decision systems, such as AI-powered hiring tools, to protect worker rights and prevent potential biases. The bill aims to address concerns about the fairness and accountability of these systems in employment decisions.
